data.title: Bitcoin exceeds $96,000 amid Senate crypto bill progress. Ethereum hits $3,327.98, XRP $2.16, Dogecoin $0.1482. Total liquidations: $680 million.
data.description: Leading cryptocurrencies experienced significant gains on Tuesday in anticipation of the Senate's markup of a bill to regulate the cryptocurrency market. Bitcoin saw a 4.43% increase, reaching $95,374.58, while Ethereum rose by 7.16% to $3,327.98. XRP surged by 5.29% to $2.16, Solana increased by 4.52% to $145.52, and Dogecoin jumped by 8.25% to $0.1482. Bitcoin reached above $96,000 for the first time in two months, with a 45% surge in trading volume in the past 24 hours. Ethereum hit a 2-month high of $3,350, and XRP and Dogecoin also experienced sharp spikes. The rally followed the Senate Banking Committee's release of a draft bill comparing altcoins to Bitcoin and Ethereum. Notably, cryptocurrency-related companies like MicroStrategy Inc. and Coinbase Global Inc. closed up by 6.63% and 4%, respectively. Additionally, nearly $680 million was liquidated from the cryptocurrency market, with $592 million in bearish shorts wiped out in the last 24 hours. For further details, the full story is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Stablecoin payment volumes on Revolut soar 156% in 2025. Most transfers fall in $100-$500 range, with customers using for medium-sized payments.
data.description: A significant number of stablecoin transfers fall within the $100 to $500 range, indicating that Revolut customers frequently utilize stablecoins for usual medium-sized transactions.

data.title: Research reveals hydrogen interaction with silicon defects creates free electrons, revolutionizing IGBT efficiency.
data.description: In Tokyo, Mitsubishi Electric Corporation, Institute of Science Tokyo, University of Tsukuba, and Quemix Corporation revealed a groundbreaking finding: they have successfully uncovered the process by which hydrogen generates free electrons by interacting with specific defects in silicon. This marks the world’s first elucidation of this phenomenon. The breakthrough holds significant promise for enhancing the design and production of insulated gate bipolar transistors (IGBTs), potentially boosting their efficiency and lowering power loss. The collaboration has opened new avenues for innovation in this field.

data.title: Fujifilm launches instax mini Link+ Smartphone Printer with enhanced features, new print modes - Business Wire.
data.description: VALHALLA, N.Y. - FUJIFILM North America Corporation, Imaging Division, has unveiled the latest addition to its instax mini Link smartphone printer series - the instax mini Link+™ (mini Link+). This innovative smartphone printer1 is equipped with enhanced instant photo technology and seamlessly integrates with the downloadable instax mini Link™ app. The app offers new features focused on inspiration, impact, and imagination, including upgraded print modes to enhance photo capture.

data.title: Fujifilm debuts instax mini Evo Cinema™ camera for personalized content with photos, 15-second videos; 3-in-1 design.
data.description: In Valhalla, N.Y., FUJIFILM North America Corporation, Imaging Division, has unveiled the new instax mini Evo Cinema™ hybrid instant camera. This innovative camera allows users to create personalized content using still photos, 15-second video clips, and instax™ mini photo prints through the instax mini Evo™ smartphone app. The 3-in-1 camera offers a dynamic and customizable experience for users.
